1.title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation

Overview *: This test plan targets the top-level validation of CCF (Central Control Fabric) power management (PM) flows within the SOC. It covers coordinated power state transitions, resource arbitration, stress detection and response, license handshake and arbitration, policy-driven flow control, and system-level observability and debug. CCF integrates with PMA, Punit, and core modules to orchestrate power state changes (C-states, frequency/voltage scaling), manage exclusive access to shared resources (MLC flush, IOSF channels), and enforce system policies. Key interactions include cross-module synchronization, telemetry-driven stress handling, license negotiation, and debug visibility, ensuring robust and safe PM flow operation under normal and stress conditions.

Scope *: This test plan encompasses validation of: (1) coordinated state transitions and resource arbitration across cores/modules, including exclusive access scenarios; (2) telemetry and AI-based stress detection and response mechanisms for electrical, thermal, and power virus events; (3) license handshake and arbitration flows between PMA, Punit, and cores; (4) policy-driven flow control and ownership prioritization; (5) system-level observability and debug mechanisms for PM flows, stress events, and arbitration outcomes. The plan addresses normal operation, stress scenarios, contention, race conditions, and system recovery. It does not cover lower-level signal integrity or individual IP-specific PM flows outside CCF orchestration.

1.1.title: Coordinated State Transitions and Resource Arbitration

Description *: Validate telemetry and AI-based detection of electrical, thermal, and power virus stress events. Trigger stress scenarios (simultaneous transitions, abnormal sensor readings) and verify that CCF/PMA responds with appropriate prevention actions (throttling, staggered transitions, aborts), including fallback and survivability flows. Observe system recovery and learning from false positives.

Validate ICCP/PVP license handshakes and arbitration across PMA, Punit, and cores. Trigger overlapping and conflicting license requests, ensuring only dominant licenses are granted, transitions are synchronized, and safe work-points are applied. Check for correct handling of license revocation and transition aborts under stress.

Validate that policy gating (Acode algorithms, security/performance policies) correctly enables/disables PM flows based on system state and stress/security conditions. Verify flow ownership and prioritization (thermal > throttle > normal), ensuring break events are handled according to defined hierarchy and module-level scope.

Validate that debug counters, status indications, and telemetry provide accurate, timely visibility into PM flows, stress events, and arbitration outcomes. Check for correct updates of status registers (e.g., SBFT_STATUS) and observability under simultaneous stress and error conditions, ensuring no loss of visibility or incorrect reporting.

Validate that CCF correctly staggers and synchronizes power state transitions (C6/MC6, frequency/voltage changes) across cores/modules, including exclusive access scenarios (MLC flush, IOSF channel arbitration). Check for correct handling of race conditions, contention, and out-of-sync status updates under normal and stress conditions.

Validate that SBFT status register and debug signals update correctly and remain observable under stress and error conditions.
Validate correct MC6 entry and MLC flush coordination, including abort handling during wake-up races.

1.1.1.1.title: Simultaneous C6 Entry and MLC Flush Race Handling

TC Description *: Configure the system with all cores active and PMA telemetry enabled. Write to THERM_STATUS_UPDATE register at address 0xA204 to simulate thermal events by toggling bits such as THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS, PROCHOT_STATUS, OUT_OF_SPEC_STATUS, THRESHOLD1_STATUS, THRESHOLD2_STATUS, POWER_LIMITATION_STATUS, CURRENT_LIMIT_STATUS, and CROSS_DOMAIN_LIMIT_STATUS. Observe per-core THERM_STATUS registers for correct sticky LOG bit behavior. Ensure that LOG bits are set on event transitions and only cleared by Ucode writes.

This test configures both cores to issue ICCP license requests with randomized legal values at the same time or in close succession, creating overlapping and conflicting scenarios. The arbitration logic is observed to ensure that the maximum requested license is always granted, and only the dominant license is applied. The test also checks that no deadlock or starvation occurs and that requests are not dropped unless superseded by a higher request within the hysteresis window.

Configure CORE_PMA_CR_TELEM_UCODE_INFO_THREAD_x[23:16] policy field to random values for all threads. Enable or disable Acode features per thread based on the policy. Monitor that all Acode features except HGS are gated according to the policy, and HGS is controlled by its dedicated knob. Verify real-time updates.

Configure the module for SBFT@Field test with both cores enabled and MLC cluster active. Ensure ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS] is at reset value 0x0. During SBFT@F execution, inject random break events such as thermal, throttle hammer, and S1/S5 CPD. Randomize test patterns and work-point parameters (voltage, frequency) within legal ranges. Continuously sample ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS], CORE_PMA_CR_IO_TAF_CFG_STATUS, and all PMA_DEBUG4 debug signals throughout the test. Verify that SBFT_STATUS transitions only through legal values and that all debug signals reflect correct sequence and state transitions, even under simultaneous stress and error injection.

Configure both cores to independently request C6 entry. Randomly trigger C6 entry on both cores with timing offsets to create contention. During MC3, inject wake-up or abort requests while MLC flush is in progress. Monitor PMA/MLC signals for flush start, abort, and flush done. Check that flush is aborted and cores exit C6 promptly if a wake-up occurs.
Test Steps *: 1. Enable PMA telemetry and ensure all cores are active. 2. Write to THERM_STATUS_UPDATE register to assert each thermal event bit individually and in combinations. 3. Sample per-core THERM_STATUS registers after each event assertion. 4. Clear events by writing '0' to the corresponding bits and observe LOG bit clearing via Ucode. 5. Repeat for all event bits and combinations.

1. Ensure both cores are active and out of C6. 2. Randomly select ICCP[3:0] values for each core within the legal range (0-4 for clients, 1-4 for servers). 3. Simultaneously or with minimal offset, set C2PmICCPRequestMXnnnH.Valid=1 and C2PmICCPRequestMXnnnH.ICCP[3:0] for both cores. 4. Monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH signals to each core and the selected license in GV CTRL (pma_iccp, pma_gv_ctrl). 5. Repeat with various overlapping/conflicting value combinations. 6. Log arbitration outcomes and check for deadlock or starvation.

1. Boot system in secured and normal flows. 2. For each thread, randomly set policy field in CORE_PMA_CR_TELEM_UCODE_INFO_THREAD_x. 3. Enable/disable Acode features per thread as dictated by policy. 4. Set HGS feature using its dedicated knob. 5. Observe Acode feature status and compare to policy field. 6. Repeat for multiple random policy values.

1. Configure module for SBFT@Field test with both cores enabled and MLC cluster active. 2. Set ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS] to reset value 0x0. 3. Start SBFT@F execution. 4. Randomly inject break events (thermal, throttle hammer, S1/S5 CPD) during test. 5. Randomize test patterns and work-point parameters (voltage, frequency) within legal ranges. 6. Continuously sample ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS] at 0x121, CORE_PMA_CR_IO_TAF_CFG_STATUS at 0x8198, and all PMA_DEBUG4 debug signals. 7. Log all status and debug signal transitions. 8. After test completion, analyze logs for correctness.

1. Enable both cores and configure for independent C6 entry. 2. Randomly trigger C6 entry on both cores with timing offsets. 3. Once both cores are in C6 (MC3), initiate MLC flush. 4. During flush, inject wake-up or abort requests to one or both cores. 5. Sample PMA/MLC signals for flush start, abort, and flush done. 6. Monitor MC3/MC6 state transitions. 7. Check for correct handling of abort and prompt C6 exit.
Pass/Fail Criteria &amp; HW Errors *: Pass if LOG bits are set on event transitions and cleared only by Ucode writes. Fail if LOG bits do not behave as specified or if events are not properly reflected in THERM_STATUS registers. HW errors include incorrect LOG bit behavior or register update failures.

Pass if the maximum requested license is always granted, only the dominant license is applied, and no deadlock or starvation occurs. Fail if a lower license is granted when a higher is requested, if both licenses are granted, or if deadlock/starvation is observed. HW errors include missed grants, incorrect GV CTRL state, or dropped requests outside the hysteresis window.
Pass if all Acode features except HGS are enabled/disabled per policy field in real-time for all threads; fail if any feature does not match policy or HGS is not controlled by its knob.

Pass if SBFT_STATUS transitions only through legal values (00, 01, 10), never 11, and all transitions are observable. Debug signals must reflect correct sequence and state transitions. Fail if any missed update, illegal value, or loss of visibility is detected.

Pass if flush is aborted upon wake-up, cores exit C6 promptly, and no deadlocks or out-of-sync states occur. Fail if flush continues after wake-up, deadlock, or incorrect state transition is observed.

Use Visa, PMON, and logs to confirm Acode feature status matches policy field and HGS knob setting.
Use logs from register reads and debug signal captures to confirm all transitions and observability.
Use PMON, logs, and Visa to confirm flush abort, C6 exit, and state transitions.

1.1.1.2.title: MLC Flush Abort During MC3 with Wake-Up Injection

TC Description *: Simulate complex thermal stress events by simultaneously asserting multiple THERM_STATUS_UPDATE bits. Monitor PMA triggers for throttling actions such as clock modulation and frequency reduction via CLPMA_CR_THROTTLE_CONFIG and PMA_CR_FORCE_THROTTLE_CONTROL. Verify system recovery when events are cleared.

This test issues ICCP license requests from both cores with conflicting values, focusing on scenarios where a lower request is made shortly after a higher one, within the hysteresis window. The test verifies that the arbitration logic does not grant the lower license until the hysteresis condition is met and that only the dominant license is applied.

Update policy field in CORE_PMA_CR_TELEM_UCODE_INFO_THREAD_x for all threads during runtime. Observe that Acode features are gated/ungated in real-time as policy changes. Verify that HGS remains controlled by its knob.

With SBFT@Field test running, ensure only one core initiates SBFT@F start command. Progress both cores through the test, ensuring that SBFT_STATUS updates only after both cores reach the required stage. Monitor MLSBFTStatusXnnnH and MLSBFTStatusValidXnnnH signals from MLC to PMA. Sample status and debug signals at each core progression point to verify correct update timing and legal value transitions.

During MC3 (all cores in C6, MLC not yet flushed), initiate MLC flush and inject wake-up events to one or both cores. Observe PM sending abort indication to MLC and MLC returning flush-done signal. Verify that flush is stopped and wake-up proceeds without deadlock.
Test Steps *: 1. Enable PMA telemetry and configure all cores. 2. Simultaneously assert multiple thermal event bits in THERM_STATUS_UPDATE register. 3. Observe PMA triggers for throttling actions. 4. Monitor system performance and recovery after clearing events. 5. Repeat with different combinations of event bits.

1. Activate both cores and ensure they are out of C6. 2. Core0 issues a high ICCP license request (e.g., License=4), Core1 issues a lower request (e.g., License=2) within the hysteresis window. 3. Set Valid=1 for both requests. 4. Monitor ICCPU2CGrantLicenseMnnnH and GV CTRL for grant behavior. 5. Repeat with varying timing and value combinations to cover hysteresis logic. 6. Log arbitration and grant timing.

1. Boot system and enable all threads. 2. Set initial policy field values. 3. During runtime, change policy field values for each thread. 4. Monitor Acode feature status for each thread after each update. 5. Check HGS feature control via knob. 6. Log feature status and compare to policy.

1. Configure SBFT@Field test with both cores enabled. 2. Start SBFT@F with only one core initiating the start command. 3. Progress both cores through test stages. 4. At each stage, sample ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS], MLSBFTStatusXnnnH, and MLSBFTStatusValidXnnnH. 5. Log all transitions and verify that updates occur only after both cores reach the required stage.

1. Set both cores to C6, enter MC3. 2. Start MLC flush. 3. Inject wake-up event to one core during flush. 4. PM sends abort indication to MLC. 5. MLC returns flush-done signal. 6. Monitor core exit from C6 and MC3. 7. Check for absence of deadlock and correct state.
Pass/Fail Criteria &amp; HW Errors *: Pass if throttling is engaged for each asserted event and system recovers when events are cleared. Fail if throttling does not occur or system fails to recover. HW errors include missed throttling triggers or recovery failures.

Pass if the lower license is not granted until the hysteresis window expires and only the dominant license is applied. Fail if the lower license is granted prematurely or both licenses are granted. HW errors include incorrect hysteresis handling or grant timing.
Pass if Acode features respond to policy changes in real-time for all threads; fail if any feature lags or does not match policy. HW errors if policy update is not reflected.
Pass if SBFT_STATUS updates only after both cores reach the stage and transitions through legal values. Fail if update occurs prematurely, with illegal value, or is missed.
Pass if flush is aborted, flush-done signal is returned, and cores exit C6 without deadlock. Fail if flush is not aborted, deadlock, or incorrect state occurs.
Intent Verify *: Use PMA logs, CLPMA_CR_THROTTLE_CONFIG and PMA_CR_FORCE_THROTTLE_CONTROL register states, and PMON to verify throttling actions.
